claims.claim: On synthetic data with known ground-truth parameters (leakage fraction 0.18, fan scale 0.94, partial tile blockage on two racks), grid-search calibration from sparse inlet sensors recovered the leakage and fan-scale parameters exactly and achieved a sensor RMSE of 0.0721 °C.

claims.claim: Inlet temperature** modeled as a weighted mixture of supply air temperature and local hot-aisle recirculation: $T_{\text{inlet}} = (1 - \lambda)\, T_{\text{supply}} + \lambda\, T_{\text{recirculation}}$, where $\lambda$ is the leakage (recirculation) fraction.

claims.claim: Tile blockage on rack $i$ is parameterized by a blockage fraction $b_i \in [0, 1]$.

evidence_refs.quote: We present a small, transparent rack-aisle thermal-airflow network model ("toy twin") designed to calibrate hidden airflow parameters from sparse rack inlet temperature sensors and rank simple cooling interventions. The model encodes rack exhaust temperature via an energy-balance relation ($Q = \dot{m}\, c_p\, \Delta T$), represents inlet temperature as a mixture of supply air and local hot-aisle recirculation, and models tile blockage, fan derating, and containment as adjustments to supply defi

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Hidden parameters (leakage fraction $\lambda$, fan scale $f$, and per-rack blockage fractions $b_i$) are calibrated by grid search over a discrete parameter space, minimizing the root-mean-square error (RMSE) between model-predicted inlet temperatures and observed inlet temperatures at a sparse set of sensor locations.
